prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: A Casserole for the Missing
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Mother Was Right All Along
Tagline: Every town has a basement.
Theme: Silence is just fear wearing cashmere.
Omen: a lake bell ringing without wind
Relationship dynamic: woman falsely accusing lovable scamp
Props: ceramic owl, boat key, blood-adjacent tea towel

Cast:
- Tess Cross | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=bakery owner | threat_aura=0.2 | space=old family office described as donation overflow | secrets=has an unsent email that would destroy a family; once left town without explaining why
- Wyatt Hale | archetype=decorative_husband | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=attorney with no visible clients | threat_aura=0.4 | space=church basement described as seasonal materials | secrets=has a storage unit he refuses to explain
- Elias Cross | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=salon owner | threat_aura=0.45 | space=retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence | secrets=once hid a casserole dish containing evidence
- Jenna Winterson | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=therapist | threat_aura=0.68 | space=storage unit described as just storage | secrets=had brunch with someone officially deceased
- Luke Hale | archetype=divorced_redemption_dad | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=marina manager | threat_aura=0.41 | space=side room with a space heater and old family records | secrets=ignored a warning text on the worst possible night; still has access to a property he does not own
- Evelyn Cross | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=innkeeper | threat_aura=0.27 | space=meditation annex nobody uses in winter | secrets=keeps screenshots like religious texts; saw the wrong person near the lake once and never shut up about it
- Luke Pike | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Noah Bell | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Wyatt Hale is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Tess Cross says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Marigold Falls.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Wyatt?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Luke. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Tess. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The ceramic owl is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Jenna."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
